pi_items.source: Verified in task.json description (line 5), setup_task.sh line 35 (rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/periop_handoff_checklist.txt), and export_result.sh line 59 (CHECKLIST_PATH=/home/ga/Desktop/periop_handoff_checklist.txt). Path is consistent across all scripts.

Verified from task.json description line 5: 'create two Pre-Op simulated devices: a Pulse Oximeter'. Confirmed as available device type in ehr_integration_feasibility/task.json metadata (line 29 lists 'Pulse Oximeter'), export_result.sh line 47 searches for device pattern 'pulse.?ox|spo2.*sim|pulse.*oximeter', and openice_architecture_assessment task description explicitly lists 'Pulse Oximeter' as a known available device type.

Verified from task.json description line 5: 'a Noninvasive Blood Pressure (NIBP) monitor'. Confirmed as available device type in icu_monitoring_setup/validated_pi.json (line 32 mentions 'NIBP monitor' as a third device option), openice_architecture_assessment task description explicitly lists 'NIBP' as a known available device type, and export_result.sh line 48 searches for device pattern 'nibp|noninvasive.*blood|blood.*pressure'.

Verified from task.json description line 5: 'create three Operating Room simulated devices: a Multiparameter Monitor'. Confirmed as available device type through multiple sources: create_simulated_device/task.json metadata line 18 specifies 'Multiparameter Monitor' as expected device type, ehr_integration_feasibility/task.json metadata line 29 lists 'Multiparameter', icu_monitoring_setup evidence shows Multiparameter Monitor creation confirmed, and export_result.sh line 51 searches for device pattern 'multiparameter|multi.?param|patient.*monitor'.

Verified from task.json description line 5: 'a Capnograph/CO2 monitor'. Confirmed as available device type in ehr_integration_feasibility/task.json metadata line 29 lists 'Capnograph', openice_architecture_assessment task description explicitly lists 'Capnograph/CO2' as a known available device type, icu_monitoring_setup task references 'CO2 or respiratory gas monitoring device', and export_result.sh line 52 searches for device pattern 'capno|co2|etco2|carbon.*dioxide'.

Verified from task.json description line 5: 'an Infusion Pump'. Confirmed as available device type through multiple sources: ehr_integration_feasibility/task.json metadata line 29 lists 'Infusion Pump', icu_monitoring_setup/validated_pi.json line 32 mentions 'infusion pump' as a third device option, openice_architecture_assessment task explicitly lists 'Infusion Pump' as a known available device type, and export_result.sh line 53 searches for device pattern 'infusion|pump|infusionpump'.

Verified from task.json description line 5: 'Launch the Vital Signs clinical application'. Confirmed as available app through multiple sources: clinical_app_tour/task.json explicitly names 'Vital Signs' as one of four available clinical demonstration applications, icu_monitoring_setup/task.json requires launching 'Vital Signs clinical application', icu_monitoring_setup/validated_pi.json line 38 verifies 'Vital Signs' as available app, fault_tolerance_test/task.json requires launching 'Vital Signs', and export_result.sh line 56 searches for app pattern 'vital.?sign|vital.*app|clinical.*app'.

Verified from task.json description requirements: 2 Pre-Op devices (Pulse Oximeter + NIBP) + 3 Operating Room devices (Multiparameter Monitor + Capnograph + Infusion Pump) = 5 total required devices. Export_result.sh collects exactly 5 device types (lines 102-108), and verifier.py device_mapping (lines 65-71) lists all 5 device keys. This is consistent with the task structure.

OpenICE uses simulated device adapters (not real medical devices). Confirmed by: (1) task.json description using term 'simulated devices', (2) create_simulated_device/task.json explicitly states these are 'simulated medical device adapters' with 'synthetic vital sign data' per validated_pi.json line 64, (3) icu_monitoring_setup description refers to 'three simulated device adapters', (4) similar tasks (create_simulated_device, icu_monitoring_setup, clinical_app_tour) all generate synthetic data from OpenICE simulators.

pi_items.verified_value: Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment (OpenICE) - real open-source platform developed by MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital (GitHub: mdpnp/mdpnp)
pi_items.source: Verified from env.json description line 5: 'OpenICE (Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment) - Medical device interoperability platform'. Confirmed as real platform through evidence_docs/README.md line 5 stating 'OpenICE is a medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP (Medical Device Plug-and-Play) program at Massachusetts General Hospital' and GitHub URL https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp. Multiple task validated_pi.json files (create_simulated_device, icu_monitoring_setup) reference the real OpenICE platform and source code repository.

privileged_info_summary: This task simulates a perioperative patient monitoring handoff using OpenICE, a real open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital (https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp). The task requires creating 5 simulated medical device adapters in two phases: Pre-Op phase with a Pulse Oximeter and NIBP monitor, and Operating Room phase with a Multiparameter Monitor, Capnograph/CO2 monitor, and Infusion Pump. The Vital Signs clinical application must be launched to confirm data flow from all devices. All data is synthetically generated by OpenICE's simulated device components, not from real patients or medical devices. The handoff checklist file must be written to /home/ga/Desktop/periop_handoff_checklist.txt documenting the devices used, monitored parameters, and transition notes.
